headline: Solitary confinement used on 340 prisoners exceeding UN limits
context: A 2024 Suhakam inspection found 340 prisoners subjected to solitary confinement exceeding the 15-day maximum recommended by the UN Mandela Rules.

cards.big: 340 prisoners held in solitary confinement exceeding UN 15-day limit
cards.sub: Suhakam found some prisoners in isolation for over 60 days continuously

cards.big: Prison Regulations 2000 permit solitary confinement as disciplinary measure
cards.sub: No maximum duration specified in Malaysian prison law for isolation

cards.big: UN Mandela Rules define solitary beyond 15 days as torture or cruel treatment
cards.sub: Malaysia's prison regulations do not align with Mandela Rules standards

cards.big: Prison officers reported using isolation due to overcrowding and staff shortages
cards.sub: Systemic issues drive practices that cause severe psychological harm

cards.big: Extended isolation damages minds and creates trauma the system must later manage
Prisons Department acknowledged need for revised solitary confinement guidelines
